rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH DICK VS. LA GALLERIA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, A NON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction To Enjoin Respondent'S Foreclosure On Petitioner'S Property
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 28, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER JOSEPH DICK'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION TO ENJOIN RESPONDENT'S FORECLOSURE ON PETITIONER'S PROPERTY The hearing will be at 10:30 a.m. in department 501. Petitioner's Motion for Preliminary Injunction to Enjoin Respondent's Foreclosure on Petitioner's Property is DENIED without prejudice. The foreclosure is not properly before the Court on the operative pleadings in this action and the Court will not be rendering a determination on the merits of the foreclosure claim in this action. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JONES MEMORIAL HOMES, INC. VS. MARK ANTHONY SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Response To Set #5 & #6 Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanciton, Supporting Declaration Of Mark Anthony Smith And Memorandum
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 28, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING RESPONSE TO SET #5 & #6 INTERROGATORIES AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS Hearing Required. In light of the supplemental opposition claiming that this action is subject to regular civil case discovery procedures and deadlines, (as opposed to summary unlawful detainer discovery procedures), the Court is prepared to treat this supplemental brief as a request to convert this unlawful detainer action into a general limited jurisdiction case; and will grant such request. As a result, this action will be removed from September 5, 2023 Unlawful Detainer trial call calendar and will be referred to department 610 for CMC calendar and trial setting. Any future motions in this action will be filed in accordance with the state and local rules pertaining to general civil matters. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
